title: BigP3BCI Study K — 9x8 adaptive/checkerboard, 2 sessions (5 healthy subjects)
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 5 healthy subjects performing a P300-based brain-computer interface (BCI) speller task using a 9x8 adaptive/checkerboard stimulus paradigm across 2 sessions. The data were acquired at 256 Hz using 16 channels with a g.USBamp amplifier and represent a derivative subset of the BigP3BCI study, the largest public P300 BCI dataset containing recordings from approximately 267 subjects across 20 studies. This dataset is suitable for P300 detection, feature extraction, and within-subject BCI performance evaluation.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired at a sampling rate of 256 Hz using 16 channels arranged in a standard 10-20 montage with a g.USBamp amplifier (g.tec). Subjects performed a P300 speller task with a 9x8 adaptive/checkerboard stimulus paradigm across 2 sessions. Visual stimuli were presented at 60 Hz line frequency, with trial intervals of 0-1.0 seconds. Target and non-target events were classified and annotated using HED 8.4.0 schema.
